meta.description: Common symptoms and their constitutional associations. A symptom may appear in multiple types but with different underlying mechanisms.
meta.warning: This is educational reference data, not a diagnostic tool. Constitutional typing requires evaluation by a licensed Korean medicine practitioner (한의사) who considers the full clinical picture.

associated_types.mechanism: Excess Yang rising to the head due to strong Lung (dispersal) pushing energy upward
Internal heat rising to the head. Often accompanied by facial flushing and irritability
Strong Liver (storage) absorbs and retains nutrients efficiently while weak Lung (dispersal) fails to expel and metabolize. This is the most weight-gain-prone type by a significant margin.
Weak Spleen (digestion) fails to generate sufficient warmth and Qi for circulation to the extremities. Core temperature is constitutionally low.
Weak Spleen means inherently poor digestive capacity. Bloating, loose stools, and poor appetite are constitutional.
Can experience digestive sluggishness due to overconsumption overwhelming the system, despite having reasonable digestive capacity
Internal heat manifests through the skin. Inflammatory acne, especially on the face and upper body.
Weak Lung function impairs skin (Lung governs skin in Korean medicine). Body acne, eczema, and slow-healing blemishes.
Excess internal heat disturbs the spirit. Mind races, difficulty calming down at night.
Strong outward energy makes it difficult to settle inward for sleep.
Weak Spleen cannot generate sufficient Qi (energy) from food. Chronic baseline low energy, worsened by cold weather or cold food.
Internal heat dries intestinal fluids. Hot, dry constipation with hard stools.
Sluggish elimination due to weak Lung (dispersal) function. More sluggish than dry.
Weak Spleen cannot properly transform and transport fluids. Cold diarrhea, especially after eating cold foods or in cold weather.
Tae-Eum types often sweat profusely with exertion. Sweating is actually therapeutic — it activates their weak Lung (dispersal) function.
Heat-driven sweating. May sweat easily even without much exertion.
Constitutional tendency toward harm-avoidance and cautious thinking. Worry depletes already low Spleen Qi, creating a negative cycle.
Weak Lung is the defining weakness of Tae-Eum. Asthma, chronic bronchitis, phlegm accumulation, and respiratory sensitivity are constitutional.
Ginseng adds extreme warmth to already maximum Yang. Severe reactions possible.
Ginseng adds heat that weak Kidney cannot cool. Headaches, insomnia, palpitations, breakouts.
